## [51:43 - 54:43]

You're building trust You're building likability. You're building loyalty. You're building all these things Which are all needed before a transaction actually happens, right? Science and buying psychology tells us that we buy from people we know like and trust And so it's absolutely important that our indoctrination process Helps us to create no ability likability and trustability with our potential client In fact, it would probably serve you well to read Robert Cialdini's book called influence if you haven't already done that And he talks about, you know, the psychological triggers that need to happen to cause someone to want to buy or transact with you And these are some of them that we're referring to here today, but it would serve you well if you go ahead and got that book So again number one the the purpose of the indoctrination piece is to indoctrinate them to you to warm them up and then two to make an offer Now hopefully an irresistible offer at that But we want to make them an offer If for most people in this training that means offering them an opportunity to get on a call with us and to perform a strategy session Now it's very important to note that this indoctrination piece Is always an extension of your magnet like you don't want to like offer a magnet about how to create a signature program For example, or how to help overcome. So first the webinar What's well, you know, what's what's good about the webinar? Well number one it kind of is like a pseudo pseudo live environment Right and there's certain things that when you're doing sales that like live is just better than non-live And so that's what we like about webinars What we also like about webinars is that they're time specific And so like if I took you to a webinar page after you're enrolled in a magnet of mine I mean, I would tell you hey look it's starting in in four days and 14 hours and 47 minutes and 48 seconds So there's a bit of urgency on there Also, when you're on a live webinar, you can make an offer that's time specific So you could say well just because you are on this call today or on this training today, you know until we end this training I'm going to include this bonus to you. So there's a lot of beautiful elements about the webinar Now there's some cons about a webinar as well. What are some of the cons? Well

## [54:43 - 57:45]

Some of the cons are if you're holding at a date and time that not everyone can make it. Well, some people might not show up Right, so that's a con Secondly, it's it's a little less automated because you got to be there live now Of course, you could do an automated webinar and um, that's almost the same thing But it doesn't quite have the punch that a live webinar does So webinar is a great indoctrination piece where you can make a great offer It has great pros and great cons and the question you got to ask is well, is it for me? Do I think that I could conduct webinars, you know, so that's one of them Second leaves a case study video. So this is a little more automated So rather than being a live environment you splash them through right to a real life case study video Now the benefits to that is that you don't actually have to be there to do the case study video, right? It's going to be held on a on a website probably lead pages or click funnels They'll see your entire case study video. They'll go through it and then they'll take the corresponding action at the end of that if they like now some of the cons are that um We get you you have to be good on video right for one, especially if you're going to do like a talking head video Where you're going to be on video Because your your personality is going to come out and if you stumble and you're not really good on video There's a con to that now on the flip side of that if you're really great on video It works heavily in your favor because people get to know your personality and see you live in an action So this is a great great sense for that right, uh one potential con is You know if it goes too long you might lose people and people will kind of jump off of it So these are some things that you want to consider Right, maybe not a case study video, but maybe you want to do a multi-part video series. I see this happen all the time Now the beauty of a multi-part video series is that a um You can break up your content in multi parts and so you can like hit them with you know 15 minutes one day And then you wait and then you know you let a day pass you have 15 minutes the other day because not everybody can Stick around for 90 minute webinar, let's say or like a 45 minute case study video or whatever So this kind of breaks it up for you and you can take each section separately. So that's kind of cool Some of the cons however are that it's harder to get people to show up to every video So maybe they'll watch your first video and a few less people watch your second video And a few less than that will watch your third and your fourth And so those are things also that you want to consider And you almost hold off on your offer until the fourth video which may work well for some people but not for others So it's a great chance to build lots of rapport, but also for a heavy potential for people to be knocked off Um number four is a trip to our sales page. In other words, you try to sell them something right away Now the benefit to this is that you can regain some of your ad cost Right, so if you're building a list and you're paying money to build that list Well, this is a way to kind of liquidate that lead cost And it allows you to capture and recapture some of those funds that you may have lost Now on the downfall to that is not everybody likes to be sold to right away And so if you kind of don't do this eloquently or elegantly

## [57:46 - 1:00:50]

What may happen is that people start seeing you as a dirty salesman or a dirty saleswoman You just they kind of be turned off by you right off the bat So some pros and cons to that one Or lastly, you can just kind of go go straight for it And uh go straight for strategy session a client of mine here was bruce and he offered um, uh A lead magnet and as soon as you splash through it was a video of saying hey It like is a very short video. It's basically like hey, I want to offer you a strategy session Now the benefit of that is that you can get on the phone with people right away I mean literally the moment you put this up and you start running an ad you will get people who are trying to book into a call with you In a matter of minutes of seeing this Uh, which is good if you're good on the phone and you can enroll them that's excellent However, the downfall is because you haven't pre indoctrinated them to you in any way shape or form These are the lowest quality leads and so when they get on the phone with you, they don't know who you are They kind of don't know what your price point is. They don't even really know what you offer If you're awesome on the phone and awesome at sales, you can overcome that But if you're not you're going to find that you're going to speak to a lot of unqualified people So that is a downfall and I just want to throw in here as as kind of um uh a note Is that the more steps you put in between? Your magnet or someone getting the magnet and for them getting on the phone with you Two things happen the more steps you do a it betters the qualification of the person because the more hoops They're willing to jump through the more qualified they are and the more interested they are to work with you So on the on the on the high point when you get on the phone with them, they're highly qualified and they're probably really excited to work with you On the downfall the more steps that you put in between someone and the magnet and getting on the phone with you the less People who are going to go through all those steps And so you have to kind of weigh those two things out Now there's no end all or be all or the best way to do this you want to factor in well What is good for my personality?
